what_it_does: Defines a browser-global cvImageStore service that persists image, project, hub panorama, and AI generation records in localStorage, downsampling image inputs to JPEG data URLs before storing them.
observations: The file is wrapped in an IIFE and attaches a single API object to window.cvImageStore.
Storage is keyed under cvstudio:cvImageStore:v1 in localStorage.
Default project records and empty scoped collections are merged with parsed stored data on load.
Images can be added from File/Blob inputs or source URLs, then downsampled through a canvas before being inserted into a scope.
The public API exposes listing, adding, updating, removing, counting, byte estimation, subscription, snapshot, and downsample operations.
AI history is capped at 60 records, while other scopes are not capped in this file.
Hub records receive yaw and hotspots fields in addition to shared image metadata.
standouts: The store centralizes multiple image surfaces behind one window-level API.
Downsampling is built into both upload and generated-source ingestion paths.
The data model separates system assets, project assets, hub panoramas, AI history, and known projects.
conventions: Uses plain browser APIs rather than modules or external dependencies.
Uses shallow immutable array replacement for collection updates.
Uses JSON cloning for snapshots and default-data copies.
Uses optional chaining for toast notification and metadata access.
Uses scope strings to route operations across collections.
concerns: localStorage write failures still notify subscribers with in-memory data that may not have persisted.
Random short IDs have no collision check.
Invalid or unsupported scope values generally produce empty results or emit without an explicit error.
Stored records are mutable through shallow list copies returned by list().
Downsampling converts all successful inputs to the configured output MIME and may discard original format details.
notes: This file is a localStorage-backed browser singleton for normalized image-library state and canvas-based image compression.
questions: Should AI-generated images also be mirrored into system, project, or hub scopes by callers?
Should project hubCount be kept in sync with actual hub records?
Should failed persistence change API return behavior or expose an error state?
Are external image source URLs expected to be CORS-compatible for canvas export?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it provides a shared image library API with scoped storage, project metadata, panorama support, AI history, persistence, and downsampling.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to provide a compact, dependency-free image substrate that multiple studio surfaces can share without each surface managing its own storage format.
novelty: Combines still image assets, 360 panorama captures, AI generation history, project records, downsampling, and subscriber notifications in one browser-global service.
contribution: Provides a fused system with a local image-store substrate: scoped asset collections, project-aware organization, panorama metadata, AI history retention, image normalization through downsampling, persistence, and a small observable API.
summary_for_embedding: Browser-global localStorage image store for ConceptV Studio that manages system, project, hub panorama, and AI image records with canvas downsampling and CRUD-style APIs.
intention_for_embedding: Created to let multiple studio surfaces share a single normalized image library and persistence layer for uploaded, generated, and panorama imagery.

symbols.does: Loads image-store state from localStorage or returns a cloned default state.
symbols.description: This function reads the configured namespace, parses stored JSON, and merges it with default collections and default projects. If storage is empty or parsing fails, it returns a fresh JSON-cloned copy of DEFAULT_DATA.

symbols.does: Writes the current data object to localStorage and notifies subscribers.
symbols.description: This function serializes the store to the namespace key and catches storage failures, surfacing a toast when available. After the write attempt, it invokes each registered listener with the data object while isolating listener errors.

symbols.does: Generates a short random image identifier prefixed with img_.
symbols.description: This helper uses Math.random(), base36 conversion, and slicing to produce IDs for new image and panorama records. It does not consult existing collections before returning an ID.

symbols.does: Converts an image input into a resized JPEG-style data URL with dimensions.
symbols.description: This async function accepts a string source or File/Blob-like input, loads it into an Image, scales it down if its long edge exceeds the configured maximum, draws it to a canvas, and returns the encoded data URL plus width and height. It defaults to 1280px long edge, 0.85 quality, and image/jpeg output, with hub callers using larger limits.
This small wrapper calls persist(data), making mutation methods share a single update-notification path. It relies on the closed-over data variable maintained by the IIFE.

symbols.does: Maps a collection, replacing the record with the matching id by a shallow merge with patch.
symbols.description: This local helper inside update() expresses record mutation as an immutable array map. Only the matching record receives the patch object; all other records are returned unchanged.

symbols.does: Filters a collection to remove the record with the matching id.
symbols.description: This local helper inside remove() creates a new array excluding any image or panorama whose id equals the requested id. It is reused for system, AI, project, and hub scopes.
